label: plain_text
text:
In Sanskrit, varga-mula (the basis, cause, origin of the square)
Was
used for square-root and ghana-mula was used for cube-root. This use of
mula for the mathematical concept of root was subsequently emulated
in Arabic and Latin through their corresponding words for the root of
a plant — jidhr and radix respectively. The term mula for root has been
used in India at least from the first century BcE. Another term used was
pada (foot, basis, cause, origin). Brahmagupta
a(628CE)
explains, 'The
pada (root) of a krti (square) is that of which it is a square.
